steps.title: Identify the exponential decay model
steps.content: When a quantity declines by a constant percentage each year, it follows exponential decay of the form $C = C_0(b)^t$. Here, $C_0 = 450$ million is the initial value in 2010, and $b$ is the yearly multiplier (what we multiply by each year).

steps.title: Convert the decline rate to a multiplier
steps.content: Declined by 12% each year means the amount left is $100\%-12\%=88\%$ of the previous year's amount, so the yearly multiplier is $0.88$. The model is $$C=450(0.88)^t.$$ That confirms B is correct.
